summary: Joel Spolsky shares a brief link to research from Australian scientists showing that open-plan offices lead to lower productivity and higher worker stress. The post reinforces his long-held position that private offices are essential for knowledge workers. By citing scientific research, he adds empirical weight to arguments he has made repeatedly throughout his blog's history. The brevity of the post suggests he views the finding as self-evident validation of his stance.
key_points: Australian scientists reviewed global research and found open-plan offices reduce productivity and increase stress
The post serves as a 'told you so' moment, reinforcing Spolsky's well-known advocacy for private offices
Scientific evidence now backs what Spolsky has argued from practical experience at Fog Creek Software
money_quotes: Australian scientists have reviewed a global pool of research into the effect of modern office design, concluding the switch to open-plan has led to lower productivity and higher worker stress.
key_insight: Scientific research confirms what Spolsky has long argued: open-plan offices harm productivity and worker well-being.
